text.title: Type-Safe Handlers and Async Services
text.summary: Axum handler pattern with typed extractors, service layer with tokio::try_join! for parallel queries, and spawn_blocking for CPU-bound work.

rule.statement: Handlers return Result<impl IntoResponse, AppError>. Extract State, Path, Query, Json as typed parameters. Validate with the validator crate before calling the service.
rule.rationale: Axum calls IntoResponse on the Ok and Err variants. A typed AppError that implements IntoResponse keeps HTTP mapping out of service code.

rule.statement: Use tokio::try_join! to run independent DB queries concurrently rather than awaiting them sequentially.
rule.rationale: Sequential awaits for count + fetch add latency proportional to one RTT. try_join! runs both in parallel and fails fast on the first error.

rule.statement: Run CPU-intensive work (Argon2, bcrypt, large serialisation) via tokio::task::spawn_blocking. Never block the async executor with synchronous CPU loops.
rule.rationale: Async runtimes use a small thread pool. A blocking call on an async thread starves other tasks waiting for their poll.

antipattern.description: Calling argon2/bcrypt hash directly inside an async fn without spawn_blocking.
antipattern.reason: Hash functions consume hundreds of milliseconds of CPU. Running them on the Tokio async thread pool starves the runtime.

rule.statement: Prefer Arc<Database> over UserService<'a> borrowing &Database. Lifetime-parameterised services fail when tokio::spawn requires 'static.
rule.rationale: tokio::spawn requires the future to be 'static. A struct that borrows a reference cannot be moved into a spawned task. Arc ownership resolves this without cloning.
